summary: A single fourslash test failed due to an assertion mismatch: the code-fix description produced by the compiler did not match the expected text in the test file.
root_cause: The fourslash test at tests/cases/fourslash/codeFixMissingTypeAnnotationOnExports52-generics-oversimplification.ts failed because an assertion expected a different code-fix message text than was produced (mismatch between 'Add return type \'Foo<string>\'' and the longer expected text).

evidence.reason: Shows there was a failing test in the run.
Identifies the failing test suite as the fourslash tests.
The exact test file that failed is printed in the log.

evidence.quote: AssertionError: expected 'Add return type \'Foo<string>\'' to equal 'Add return type \'Foo<string, string[…'
evidence.reason: The assertion failure shows the produced code-fix message ('Add return type \'Foo<string>\'') did not match the expected message (the longer 'Add return type \'Foo<string, string[…').
The command used to run tests (useful for reproducing the failure locally).

suggested_fix: Either update the expected code-fix description text in tests/cases/fourslash/codeFixMissingTypeAnnotationOnExports52-generics-oversimplification.ts to match the actual message 'Add return type \'Foo<string>\'', or change the code-fix implementation to produce the originally expected message. Re-run the test suite locally with: npm test -- --no-lint --coverage to verify the fix.
